title: The Day The Earth Stood Stupid

jokes.text: Nibbler, roll over and you get a ham. Roll over. Roll over for the ham.
jokes.punchline: Repetitive, desperate pleading for a simple trick
jokes.explanation: Simple pet owner desperation, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: - Five hundred dollars, you say? - Dog food, you say?
jokes.punchline: Each character focuses on what matters to them most - money vs food
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beats - Bender's greed and Zoidberg's hunger. Economical parallel structure works well.

jokes.text: I have a late entry. My hard-shelled whooping terrier, Mr. Zoidberg.
jokes.setup: Dog show with typical pet breeds
jokes.punchline: Passing off an alien lobster creature as a dog breed
jokes.explanation: Absurd fake dog breed name combined with treating Zoidberg as a pet. Creative and character-appropriate for Bender's schemes.

jokes.text: Suck in that gut! You wanna be spayed?
jokes.punchline: Threatening neutering as motivation
jokes.explanation: Dark threat fits Bender's cruel personality perfectly. The spaying threat is unexpectedly harsh for pet training.

jokes.text: I should be weeping! I'm not weeping!
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg announcing his own emotional state while being treated as a pet
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's self-awareness of his degrading situation creates cringe comedy

jokes.text: ...Dave Spiegel and his owner, Fluffers.
jokes.punchline: Reversing who owns whom - the pet owns the human
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection joke with perfect economy

jokes.text: Second prize, this lovely afghan, made from an Afghan, goes to...
jokes.setup: Typical prize announcement at dog show
jokes.punchline: Afghan blanket literally made from a person from Afghanistan
jokes.explanation: Clever dark wordplay on afghan/Afghan. Economical and memorable, though morally questionable which fits Futurama's dark humor.

jokes.text: Second place? That's a fancy word for losing! You didn't stick your landing!
jokes.punchline: Bender's sore loser attitude and blaming Zoidberg
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - ungrateful and abusive to his 'pet'. The gymnastics reference is oddly specific for a dog show.

jokes.text: That's the eloquence you'd expect from the owner of...the Dumbest Pet in Show!
jokes.punchline: Backhanded award for dumbest pet
jokes.explanation: Good structure building to the embarrassing reveal. Cringe humor at Leela's expense.

jokes.text: All glory to the Hypno-Toad!
jokes.punchline: Everyone mindlessly chanting praise
jokes.explanation: Iconic Futurama running gag. Became a cultural meme. Perfect absurdist concept of hypnotic amphibian overlord.

jokes.text: Don't listen to them. People said I was dumb, but I proved them.
jokes.punchline: Trails off mid-sentence, proving he's actually dumb
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - accidentally proving the opposite of his point. Great structure and character integration.

jokes.text: - Why is that good news? - They paid in advance.
jokes.setup: Planet destroyed, Professor calls it good news
jokes.punchline: Only cares about getting paid despite planetary genocide
jokes.explanation: Dark capitalism humor. Professor's amoral business sense trumps basic human decency.

jokes.text: - The planet we live on? - I'd hate to be those guys.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Earth being next to be destroyed
jokes.punchline: Fry doesn't realize they ARE 'those guys'
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry obliviousness. Good structure of setup/punchline revealing his lack of awareness.

jokes.text: What's got into him? He's twitching like Zoidberg when you say 'food.'
jokes.punchline: Comparison to Zoidberg's food obsession
jokes.explanation: References established Zoidberg trait. Decent character-based comparison.

jokes.text: What, now?
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg immediately demonstrates the behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback timing. Zoidberg proves Leela's point instantly. Economical and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Am I going crazy? Have my years of hedonism caught up with me?
jokes.setup: Leela seeing Nibbler do impossible things
jokes.punchline: Blames her own supposed wild lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Ironic since Leela is relatively responsible. Good character moment of self-doubt.

jokes.text: - Help me! My heart stopped! - You don't have one. You're a robot.
jokes.punchline: Fry points out logical impossibility
jokes.explanation: Basic robot logic humor. Fry being the voice of reason is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: - Well, yeah. Robots are made of metal. - Am I a robot?
jokes.punchline: Bender has existential crisis about his own nature
jokes.explanation: Absurd that Bender would question his own robot nature. Shows the brain effects starting.

jokes.text: Zoidbee want balloon! Want balloon now! Zoidbee want go outside!
jokes.punchline: Reverts to childlike baby talk
jokes.explanation: Baby talk is cringeworthy and shows brain effects. 'Zoidbee' nickname is endearing.

jokes.text: Unbelievable! I thought you were a furry little moron. But here you are flying an adorable spaceship!
jokes.punchline: Still calls his spaceship 'adorable' despite the revelation
jokes.explanation: Leela's maternal instincts override the shock of discovery. The 'adorable spaceship' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Oh, stupider.
jokes.setup: Nibbler explains brains make everyone stupid
jokes.punchline: Casual correction that they make people stupider, implying humans were already dumb
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery and devastating implication. Economical and brutally effective insult to humanity.

jokes.text: So every religion is wrong.
jokes.setup: Nibbler explains the beginning of the universe
jokes.punchline: Leela's matter-of-fact conclusion about religion
jokes.explanation: Bold religious commentary delivered casually. Economical and provocative.

jokes.text: I'm sending in more trains.
jokes.punchline: Governor's solution is just more trains
jokes.explanation: Absurd government logic under stupification. Illogical but confident solution.

jokes.text: How do you say the letter that looks like a man with a hat?
jokes.punchline: Describes the letter 'T' in the most roundabout way possible
jokes.explanation: Creative visual description of a letter. Shows alien perspective combined with stupification effects.

jokes.text: Let's buy Internet stock! On margin! Zoidbee wanna buy on margin!
jokes.setup: Characters acting stupid and making bad decisions
jokes.punchline: References to dot-com bubble and risky investment strategies
jokes.explanation: Good satire of financial bubble mentality. Shows how stupification manifests as bad decisions.

jokes.text: Hey! Let's all join the Reform Party!
jokes.punchline: Political party joke implying Reform Party membership indicates stupidity
jokes.explanation: Dated political reference. Moderately effective as example of stupid decision-making.

jokes.text: By the time you pronounce one letter of my name...a trillion cosmoses would sink into eternal night.
jokes.punchline: Cosmic-scale exaggeration of how long his name is
jokes.explanation: Excellent cosmic-scale absurdity. Creative way to avoid having to create an alien name.

jokes.text: When the universe was forged in the big bang...our mighty race was already 17 years old.
jokes.setup: Ancient race present at universe's beginning
jokes.punchline: Only 17 years old, which is hardly ancient
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke. Sets up expectation of eons, delivers mundane teenage years.

jokes.text: Thoughts screech at them like the forced laughs of movie patrons.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Brain Spawn hate consciousness
jokes.punchline: Comparison to fake laughter in bad movies
jokes.explanation: Sophisticated simile connecting cosmic horror to mundane social experience. Meta-commentary on entertainment.

jokes.text: Wipe out thought? My God! They're flying televisions!
jokes.setup: Brain Spawn want to eliminate thought
jokes.punchline: Comparison to television's mind-numbing effects
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary. Economical connection between alien threat and media criticism.

jokes.text: Like a carpet-remnant prom dress. - Like your prom dress.
jokes.setup: Nibbler explaining Fry's mind with elaborate metaphor
jokes.punchline: Leela taking it as personal insult about her prom dress
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's insecurity despite cosmic stakes

jokes.text: - Like a carpet-remnant prom dress. - Like your prom dress.
jokes.punchline: Leela takes it personally as an insult to her own prom dress
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's insecurity. Nice back-and-forth structure.

jokes.text: - A pickle jar? - Thomas Jefferson?
jokes.setup: Question about first president George Washington
jokes.punchline: Two equally wrong but different types of wrong answers
jokes.explanation: Shows different types of stupification - random (pickle jar) vs historically confused (wrong president).

jokes.text: Oh, Chester A. Arthur fall down.
jokes.punchline: References obscure president in baby talk
jokes.explanation: Clever that Professor's stupified state still retains obscure historical knowledge, just expressed childishly.

jokes.text: A giant brain is basically a giant nerd. And where would a giant nerd be? The library!
jokes.setup: Need to find the giant brain
jokes.punchline: Fry's surprisingly logical deduction based on nerd stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Fry being accidentally brilliant through simple logic. Good structure building to the reveal.

jokes.text: Dewey, you fool, your decimal system has played right into my hands.
jokes.punchline: Blames library organization system for making absorption easier
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about library science. Makes Dewey Decimal System sound like evil plot. Original and clever.

jokes.text: We have long since evolved beyond the need for asses.
jokes.setup: Fry threatens to kick his ass
jokes.punchline: Brain takes it literally about anatomical evolution
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on ass-kicking idiom. Brain's literal interpretation fits character.

jokes.text: That's right! You'll find a little knowledge is a dangerous thing!
jokes.punchline: Misquotes famous saying, proving he has little knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - confidently misquoting while proving the point. Excellent irony and character integration.

jokes.text: Hardy Boys? Too easy. Nancy Drew? Too hard. Perfect! Bonfire of the Vanities!
jokes.setup: Fry trying to think of books to hurt the brain
jokes.punchline: Choosing notoriously difficult Tom Wolfe novel as 'just right'
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with literary knowledge - Bonfire as 'perfect' difficulty is funny

jokes.text: - Sometimes I fear we are cute. - Oh, niggle-snooch.
jokes.setup: Trying to be fearsome warrior race
jokes.punchline: Worried about being too adorable, confirmed by cute nonsense word
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between their serious mission and inherent adorability. 'Niggle-snooch' is perfect cute alien word.

jokes.text: - Big whale over there. - I saw it first.
jokes.setup: In Moby Dick world, crew spots the white whale
jokes.punchline: Treating whale hunting like a game with childish competition
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor deflating serious literary moment

jokes.text: Wait! That no white whale! It gray thinkie whale!
jokes.setup: Inside Moby Dick, looking for white whale
jokes.punchline: Bender's stupid description of brain as 'gray thinkie whale'
jokes.explanation: 'Thinkie whale' is delightfully childish description. Shows Bender's stupified state while advancing plot.

jokes.text: Tom Sawyer, you tricked me. This is less fun than previously indicated.
jokes.setup: Bender doing fence painting in Tom Sawyer
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's been conned like the original story
jokes.explanation: Good reference to classic literature trick. Bender's formal phrasing while stupid is amusing.

jokes.text: A bachelor at your age. - I'm an oddity? Wait till...
jokes.setup: In Pride and Prejudice world, commenting on Fry being unmarried
jokes.punchline: Fry about to reveal something even stranger about himself
jokes.explanation: Setup for larger joke about Fry's oddity in literary context

jokes.text: I'm a gigantic brain!
jokes.setup: Big Brain trying to blend in at Pride and Prejudice ball
jokes.punchline: States the obvious about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Simple absurd honesty. Brain's inability to blend in despite literary setting.

jokes.text: I'm a gigantic brain! / I say! Most ungentlemanlike.
jokes.setup: Brain reveals himself in Jane Austen setting
jokes.punchline: Treated as merely a breach of etiquette
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition humor - cosmic horror treated as social impropriety

jokes.text: The Big Brain am winning again! I am the 'greetest'!
jokes.punchline: Poor grammar and made-up superlative show he's been affected by his own trap
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic reversal - the brain meant to make others stupid has become stupid himself. 'Greetest' is memorably wrong.

jokes.text: Now I am leaving Earth for no 'raisin'!
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'reason' as 'raisin'
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective malapropism showing brain's stupified state. Quotable mispronunciation.

jokes.text: And so, life returned to normal. Or as normal as it gets on this dirtball inhabited by psychotic apes.
jokes.punchline: Describes Earth and humans in brutally honest terms
jokes.explanation: Dark but accurate assessment of humanity from alien perspective. Economical insult delivery.

jokes.text: Except Fry. And no one believed him or cared what he had to say.
jokes.punchline: Nobody believes the hero who saved the world
jokes.explanation: Sad irony perfect for Fry's character - saves world but gets no credit. Classic unsung hero trope.

jokes.text: And when that day comes, God help us. God help us all.
jokes.punchline: Appeals to God despite being more advanced than any religion
jokes.explanation: Ironic given earlier establishment that all religions are wrong. Dark ending appropriate for alien perspective.

jokes.text: Time for a diapie change.
jokes.setup: Serious alien warning about future threats
jokes.punchline: Cut to Leela treating Nibbler like a baby again
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending irony - return to status quo where cosmic being is treated as pet. Great structural contrast.
